description: city in and county seat of Humboldt County, California, United States
description_wiki: Eureka ( yuurr-EE-kə; Wiyot: Jaroujiji; Hupa: dahwilahłding; Karok: Uuth) is a city in and the county seat of Humboldt County, located on the North Coast of California.

description: county in California, United States
description_wiki: Humboldt County ( ) is the westernmost county in the U.S. state of California.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: commune in Haute-Vienne, France
description_wiki: Saint-Sylvestre (French pronunciation: [sɛ̃ silvɛstʁ] ; Occitan: Sint Sauvestre) is a commune in the Haute-Vienne department in the Nouvelle-Aquitaine region in west-central France.

description: part of the Alps mountain range in France
description_wiki: The French Alps are the portions of the Alps mountain range that stand within France, located in the Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes and Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur regions.

description: commune in Hautes-Alpes, France
description_wiki: Gap (French pronunciation: [ɡap], Occitan: [ˈɡap]) is the prefecture of the department of Hautes-Alpes, in the region of Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, southern France.

description: multiple-stage bicycle race primarily held in France
description_wiki: The Tour de France (pronounced [tuʁ də fʁɑ̃s]) is an annual men's multiple-stage road cycling race held primarily in France.

description: sea between Europe, Africa and Asia
description_wiki: The Mediterranean Sea ( MED-ih-tə-RAY-nee-ən) is an intercontinental sea situated between Europe, Asia, and Africa.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

description_wiki: Idaho ( , EYE-də-hoe) is a landlocked state in the Pacific Northwest and Mountain West subregions of the Western United States.

description_wiki: Utah is a landlocked state in the Mountain West subregion of the  Western United States.

description_wiki: Colorado is a landlocked state in the Mountain West subregion of the Western United States.

description: town in Sublette County, Wyoming, United States
description_wiki: Big Piney is a town in Sublette County, Wyoming, United States.

description: city in Bannock and Power counties in Idaho, United States, that is the county seat of Bannock County
description_wiki: Pocatello ( ) is the county seat of and the largest city in Bannock County, with a small portion on the Fort Hall Indian Reservation in neighboring Power County, containing the city's airport.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: California () is a state in the Western United States that lies on the Pacific Coast.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: census-designated place in Humboldt County, California
description_wiki: Fieldbrook (formerly Bokman's Prairie and Buckman's Prairie) is a census-designated place in Humboldt County, California.

description: land warfare branch of France's military
description_wiki: The French Army (French: Armée de terre, lit. 'Army of Land') is the land service branch of the French Armed Forces.

description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: American coffee brand
description_wiki: Folgers is an American brand of coffee produced and sold in the United States, with additional distribution in Asia, Canada and Mexico.

description_wiki: Harry Bridges (28 July 1901 – 30 March 1990) was an Australian-born American union leader, first with the International Longshoremen's Association (ILA).

description: city in Humboldt County, California, United States
description_wiki: Arcata (; Wiyot: Goudi’ni; Yurok: Oket'oh) is a city adjacent to the Arcata Bay (northern) portion of Humboldt Bay in Humboldt County, California, United States.

entity: Lumber and Sawmill Workers' Union, Local 2789

description: labor union
description_wiki: The International Longshore and Warehouse Union (ILWU) is a labor union which primarily represents dock workers on the West Coast of the United States, Hawaii, and in British Columbia, Canada; on the East Coast, the dominant union is the International Longshoremen's Association.

description: seat of Multnomah County, and largest city in state of Oregon, United States
description_wiki: Portland ( PORT-lənd) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Oregon.
